title: Digital Connection vs. Mental Well-being
content: Title slide introducing the presentation topic on the relationship between digital connection and mental well-being. Presented by Li Wei, a Year 2 Sociology student, for Global Studies 201.

title: The Ubiquitous Landscape
content: This slide describes Gen Z as Digital Natives who experience life through an online lens, supported by usage statistics of major social media platforms. It emphasizes that constant connectivity has become an indispensable part of modern life.
key_claims: Gen Z are recognized as Digital Natives, experiencing life through an online lens
70% of students engage with Instagram and Snapchat
Constant connectivity is a fundamental and indispensable reality in the modern era

title: Usage and Anxiety Statistics
content: This slide presents a bar chart comparing social media usage rates with mental health outcomes among users, citing data from the Mental Health Statistics Report 2025.
key_claims: 87% of users spend more than 2 hours per day on social media
There is a 70% increase in depression risk factor

title: Psychological Manifestations
content: This slide outlines three key psychological effects of social media use on individuals: FOMO, social comparison, and cyberbullying, each explained with a brief description.
key_claims: FOMO Effect: fear of missing out creates significant stress and anxiety regarding social inclusion
Comparison: constant exposure to curated feeds leads to negative self-image and low self-esteem
Cyberbullying: nearly 87% of teens have experienced some form of online harassment or negative interactions

title: Strategic Mitigation Efforts
content: This slide proposes three strategies to address the negative mental health impacts of social media use, focusing on individual and corporate responsibility.
key_claims: Digital Detox: implementing time limits is a crucial strategy for emotional balance
Awareness: education regarding platform algorithms is essentially significant
Platform Responsibility: companies should prioritize user well-being as a primary imperative
